rule: Use React.memo for expensive components
description: Wrap components in React.memo when they receive the same props frequently. Combine with useCallback for handlers.

rule: Stabilize event handlers with useCallback
description: Always wrap handlers in useCallback when passed to memoized children to prevent unnecessary rerenders.

rule: Memoize expensive calculations with useMemo
description: Use useMemo for computationally expensive derivations. Not needed for simple values.

rule: Lazy-load route components with React.lazy
description: Split code by route using React.lazy + Suspense. Reduces initial bundle significantly.
tags: react lazy loading code splitting routes

rule: Add Suspense boundaries with skeleton fallbacks
description: Wrap lazy components in Suspense with skeleton fallback to prevent blank screens.

rule: Include all dependencies in React Query keys
description: Query keys must include all variables the query depends on (userId empresaId etc) for proper cache isolation.
tags: react query tanstack cache keys isolation

rule: Invalidate queries after mutations
description: Always call queryClient.invalidateQueries after mutations to keep UI in sync.

rule: Use optimistic updates for instant UI feedback
description: Update cache immediately on mutation then rollback on error for snappy UX.
tags: react query optimistic update instant ui

rule: Use stable unique keys in lists
description: Never use array index as key. Use IDs or stable unique identifiers.

rule: Avoid anonymous functions in JSX props
description: Extract handlers outside JSX to prevent recreation on every render.

rule: Colocate state close to where it is used
description: Don't lift state higher than necessary. Keep state in the smallest component that needs it.
example: // Keep dialog open state in the Dialog component, not parent

rule: Split contexts by update frequency
description: Separate rarely-updated contexts (auth theme) from frequently-updated ones (selection filters).

rule: Prefer design tokens over arbitrary values
description: Use Tailwind's built-in scale (p-4 gap-6) not arbitrary [padding:17px]. Consistency over precision.
example: Use p-4 not p-[17px]. Use text-sm not text-[13px].

rule: Use CSS variables for semantic colors
description: Define --primary --secondary --accent as CSS custom properties in index.css. Use them in tailwind.config.
tags: tailwind semantic colors css variables theming

rule: Configure dark mode with class strategy
description: Use class-based dark mode for explicit toggling not media query.
example: darkMode: 'class' in tailwind.config; add 'dark' class to <html>

rule: Use cn() utility for conditional classes
description: Use clsx + tailwind-merge via cn() helper to merge conditional classes safely.

rule: Use cva() for component variants
description: Use class-variance-authority for components with multiple visual variants.
example: const buttonVariants = cva('base', { variants: { size: { sm: '...', md: '...' } } })

rule: Use group and peer for dependent styles
description: Parent hover affects children (group) or sibling state affects sibling (peer).

rule: Always use installed shadcn components
description: Before creating a new component check if shadcn/ui already has it. 47 components installed.
example: Use <Dialog> not custom modal. <Select> not custom dropdown.

rule: Compose shadcn primitives for custom components
description: Build complex components by composing shadcn primitives rather than from scratch.

rule: Use Form component with react-hook-form
description: Shadcn Form integrates react-hook-form. Use FormField FormControl FormMessage.

rule: Use Sonner for toast notifications
description: Project uses Sonner (sonner package) for toasts. Use toast.success() toast.error() etc.

rule: Use Command for search/autocomplete
description: For combobox or search-with-suggestions use shadcn Command component.

rule: Use Sheet for side panels and drawers
description: For slide-in panels from the side use shadcn Sheet not custom drawers.

rule: Use Dialog for modals
description: For modals use shadcn Dialog. Use AlertDialog for destructive confirmations.

rule: Use Skeleton for loading states
description: Replace spinners with Skeleton component for content-shaped loading placeholders.

rule: Use Badge for status indicators
description: For status labels chips tags use shadcn Badge with semantic variants.

rule: Use Table for data grids
description: For structured data use shadcn Table with proper thead/tbody/tfoot. Add sorting via column headers.

rule: Use Tabs for content switching
description: For multi-section views use shadcn Tabs. Don't build custom tab implementations.

rule: Use Popover for rich dropdowns
description: For dropdowns with custom content (not simple select) use Popover.

rule: Use focus-visible not focus for focus styles
description: focus-visible only shows focus ring on keyboard navigation not mouse click.

rule: Use aria-live for dynamic content announcements
description: Announce dynamic content updates to screen readers with aria-live regions.

rule: Use semantic HTML elements
description: Use button for clicks nav for navigation main article section aside etc.

rule: Add skip to main content link
description: First focusable element should be a skip link for keyboard users.

rule: Always handle loading empty and error states
description: Every async data fetch needs loading skeleton empty state and error state.
tags: web loading empty error states ux

rule: Implement optimistic UI for mutations
description: Update UI immediately on user action then confirm with server. Rollback on error.
example: Toggle checkbox immediately then call API in background

rule: Debounce search inputs and API calls
description: Add 300-500ms debounce to search inputs to prevent excessive API calls.

rule: Virtualize long lists
description: For lists >50 items use react-window or @tanstack/virtual to virtualize.
example: Only renders visible items not all 1000+ rows

rule: Use optimized image formats and lazy loading
description: Use WebP/AVIF formats declare width/height to prevent CLS use loading='lazy' for below fold.
tags: web image optimization performance cls lazy

rule: Mobile-first responsive breakpoints
description: Design mobile first then add responsive modifiers sm: md: lg: xl:

rule: Use container queries for component-level responsiveness
description: For components that respond to their container (not viewport) use @container.

rule: Define design tokens as CSS custom properties
description: Use :root CSS custom properties for all design tokens colors spacing etc.
tags: web css variables custom properties tokens theming

rule: Wrap key sections in Error Boundaries
description: Use React Error Boundaries to gracefully handle runtime errors without crashing the whole app.

rule: Use CSS transitions not JS animations when possible
description: CSS transitions are GPU-accelerated. Use Tailwind transition classes for simple animations.

rule: Export className prop in all custom components
description: Always accept and forward className prop in custom components for composability.
example: function MyCard({ className, ...props }) { return <div className={cn('base', className)} />
